🗺️

Driving Directions

BZE → Placencia
📍 Open in Google Maps Note: Google Maps may suggest the unpaved Coastal Highway — use the routes below instead.

Route 1 — Recommended · ~3 to 3.5 hrs

From BZE, head north briefly on the Northern Highway, then turn left onto Burrell Boom Road to bypass Belize City. Merge right onto the George Price Highway (Western Hwy) — pass the Belize Zoo around Mile 30. Continue west to Belmopan, then turn left onto the Hummingbird Highway (follow signs for Dangriga / Punta Gorda / Placencia). This is the most scenic stretch — winding through the Maya Mountains, jungle hills, and small villages. As you approach Dangriga, look for the Shell Gas Station on your right, then turn right onto the Southern Highway. Follow for ~35 min to the Placencia roundabout, take the left, and follow the Peninsula Road through Seine Bight into Placencia Village.

Belize Zoo · Maya Mountains · Hummingbird Highway scenery · Small villages

Watch for speed bumps in villages. Road is well-paved and well-signed. Allow extra time if stopping.

Route 2 — Full Hummingbird · ~3.5 to 4 hrs · Most Scenic

Follow the same start as Route 1 (Burrell Boom Road → George Price Hwy) all the way west to Belmopan. Turn left and take the full length of the Hummingbird Highway from Belmopan to Dangriga — this maximizes time on Belize's most celebrated road, winding deeper through the Cayo District valleys and Maya Mountain foothills. Stops along the way include St. Herman's Blue Hole National Park (swimming hole & caves) and views over jungle canopy. Rejoin the Southern Highway at Dangriga and follow to Placencia as above.

Full Hummingbird Hwy · St. Herman's Blue Hole · Cayo valleys · Dangriga (Garifuna cultural capital)

Add 30–45 min vs. Route 1. Best if departing BZE before noon. Consider a stop at Blue Hole for a swim — it's worth it.

⚠️ Avoid the Coastal Highway — despite appearing on maps as a shortcut, it is an unpaved dirt road. Crystal Auto Rental explicitly prohibits use of rental vehicles on it.
